## [06:04 - 09:04]

You know, obviously different people do things differently. I usually just co-call the supplier and get the contact details. And this is what I normally do. When you're dealing with suppliers or co-calling in general, it's very important to create a bit of a report between you and them. For example, whenever I call them, I always begin something like this. Hey, my name is Richard and I'm from an Australian luggage brand called July. I met you guys at Canton Fair last year and someone gave me your business card. And I like to speak to someone who look after an Australian market. Or you can say that someone recommended them to you. And maybe they'll come back and say, hey, I don't know because we never attended. You can just say, hey, don't worry about it. It could be another fair or it could be another event. But most important thing is you create that kind of report so they know you and you can start chatting to them. The next step is to grab the contact number or the WeChat account so you can chat further. Because sometimes there's a language barrier. It's very hard to explain everything over the phone.
